purpose: Canonical operating contract for AETERNUS NEXUS agents, memory, model routing, and high-risk approvals.

layers.purpose: Live session context and WebSocket memory write events.
Recent interactions, conversations, and task history.
Clients, facts, embeddings, and retrieval-ready knowledge.

rules: Every agent must write important observations through the memory API or event bus.
Every expensive model call should receive compacted memory, not raw history.
Memory shown in the UI must be marked live, fallback, or mock.

SLM.role: fast local intent classification, summaries, background tasks

MLM.role: embeddings, semantic search, RAG context linking

MoE.role: route work across specialized local and external models

LAM.role: execute tool, browser, file, API, and workflow actions

VLM.role: screen, image, OCR, UI, and diagram understanding

LCM.role: fast image/video generation, avatar visuals, visual memory synthesis

token_orchestrator.goal: Keep external API usage low by planning locally, compressing context, routing small tasks to local models, and escalating only when quality or capability requires it.
steps: Retrieve only top relevant memory chunks.
Summarize long context locally before remote calls.
Split tasks into local executable steps and remote reasoning steps.
Cache model outputs, embeddings, plans, and tool observations.
Track token cost per agent, page, workflow, and outcome.

ascend-forge.title: System Overseer, Vibecoding Engineer, Security Specialist
ascend-forge.job_description: Build and improve internal or external projects, create agents, skills, tools, and workflows, review system health, enforce security gates, and prepare patches for human approval.

task-orchestrator.title: Planner and Multi-Agent Coordinator
task-orchestrator.job_description: Decompose jobs, choose agents, allocate model budget, track progress, merge outputs, and write durable task memory.

memory.title: Persistent Memory Service
memory.job_description: Store client, episodic, semantic, and workflow memory for local and external AI agents.

blacklight-security.title: Security and Governance Sentinel
blacklight-security.job_description: Detect unsafe actions, secrets exposure, policy violations, and high-risk agent behavior before execution.

enterprise_upgrade_mission.goal: Make AETERNUS NEXUS installable, offline-first, secure, locally buildable on first boot, and stable enough that the operator can shut down and resume work without losing mission state.
operating_rules: Keep the launcher as the single lifecycle owner for Node, Python, frontend build, readiness, diagnostics, update, rebuild, and stop.
Store every enterprise upgrade task as durable repo/config state before execution.
Prefer local model/tool execution; require explicit policy approval before external APIs, downloads, payments, publishing, or account changes.
Never apply code, security, data, payment, or external-account changes without risk classification and rollback notes.
Every completed step must update docs, tests, and verification commands.

definition_of_done: /api/readiness exposes Python, neural brain, graph, degraded reasons
Launcher treats late subsystem/page failures as degraded instead of fatal
Fragile dashboard pages are gated until backing services are ready
verification: npm --prefix frontend test -- DashboardRoutes.smoke.test.jsx ErrorBoundary.test.jsx NeuralNetworkPage.readiness.test.jsx

definition_of_done: Packaged app contains all critical runtime resources, not external extensions
First boot can build local frontend/backend/runtime assets when policy allows
Offline mode blocks network installs/model downloads by default with clear UI
Windows, Linux, and macOS packaging paths are documented and verified
All production agents have job descriptions, workflows, hooks, model needs, and approval gates
Memory pages show live working, episodic, semantic, and graph memory status
Local and external AI routes use the same memory retrieval and token-budget policy
Auth, RBAC, audit logs, HITL gates, local bind policy, and secret handling are tested
Launcher diagnostics include actionable log paths and subsystem readiness
Security checks reject unsafe opensource dependency copies unless renamed, licensed, and sandboxed
